nodes.label: ∅ | rostam | ∅
nodes.sentence:
Rostam and Sohrab
Give ear unto the combat of Sohrab against Rostam, though it be a tale replete with tears.

nodes.label: it | come | that
nodes.sentence: It came about that on a certain day Rostam arose from his couch, and his mind was filled with forebodings.

nodes.label: he | bethink | he
nodes.sentence: He bethought him therefore to go out to the chase.

nodes.label: he | saddle | rakhsh
nodes.sentence: So he saddled Rakhsh and made ready his quiver with arrows.

nodes.label: he | turn | he
nodes.sentence: Then he turned him unto the wilds that lie near Turan, even in the direction of the city of Samengan.

nodes.label: he | start | herd
nodes.sentence: And when he was come nigh unto it, he started a herd of asses and made sport among them till that he was weary of the hunt.

nodes.label: he | catch | one
nodes.sentence: Then he caught one and slew it and roasted it for his meal, and when he had eaten it and broken the bones for the marrow, he laid himself down to slumber, and Rakhsh cropped the pasture beside him.

nodes.label: ∅ | pass | ∅
nodes.sentence: Now while the hero was sleeping there passed by seven knights of Turan, and they beheld Rakhsh and coveted him.

nodes.label: they | throw | cord
nodes.sentence: So they threw their cords at him to ensnare him.

nodes.label: rakhsh | paw | ground
nodes.sentence: But Rakhsh, when he beheld their design, pawed the ground in anger, and fell upon them as he had fallen upon the lion.

nodes.label: they | ensnare | he
nodes.sentence: So they ensnared him and led him into the city, thinking in their hearts, "Verily a goodly capture have we made."

nodes.label: rostam | be | ∅
nodes.sentence:
But Rostam when he awoke from his slumbers was downcast and sore grieved when he saw not his steed, and he said unto himself-

"How can I stand against the Turks, and how can I traverse the desert alone?"

nodes.label: heart | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: And his heart was full of trouble.

nodes.label: he | seek | trace
nodes.sentence: Then he sought for the traces of the horse's hoofs, and he followed them, and they led him even unto the gates of the city.

nodes.label: rostam | tell | they
nodes.sentence: Then Rostam told them how Rakhsh was vanished while he slumbered, and how he had followed his track even unto these gates.

nodes.label: he | sware | oath
nodes.sentence: And he sware a great oath, and vowed that if his courser were not restored unto him many heads should quit their trunks.

nodes.label: king | speak | word
nodes.sentence:
Then the King of Samengan, when he saw that Rostam was beside himself with anger, spoke words of soothing, and said that none of his people should do wrong unto the hero; and he begged him that he would enter into his house and abide with him until that search had been made, saying-

"Surely Rakhsh cannot be hid."

nodes.label: rostam | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: And Rostam was satisfied at these words, and cast suspicion from his spirit, and entered the house of the King, and feasted with him, and beguiled the hours with wine.

nodes.label: king | rejoice | guest
nodes.sentence: And the King rejoiced in his guest, and encompassed him with sweet singers and all honour.

nodes.label: king | lead | rostam
nodes.sentence: And when the night was fallen the King himself led Rostam unto a couch perfumed with musk and roses, and he bade him slumber sweetly until the morning.

nodes.label: he | declare | he
nodes.sentence: And he declared to him yet again that all was well for him and for his steed.

nodes.label: ∅ | step | slave
nodes.sentence: And there stepped within a slave bearing a lamp perfumed with amber, and a woman whose beauty was veiled came after her.

nodes.label: ∅ | scatter | robe
nodes.sentence: And as she moved musk was scattered from her robes.

nodes.label: woman | come | bed
nodes.sentence: And the women came nigh unto the bed of the hero heavy with wine and slumber.

nodes.label: he | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: And he was amazed when he saw them.

nodes.label: he | spake | ∅
nodes.sentence:
And when he had roused him somewhat he spake and said-

"Who art thou, and what is thy name and thy desire, and what seekest thou from me in the dark night?

nodes.label: faced | answer | he
nodes.sentence:
"

Then the Peri-faced answered him, saying, "I am Tahmineh, the daughter of the King of Samengan, of the race of the leopard and the lion, and none of the princes of this earth are worthy of my hand, neither hath any man seen me unveiled.

nodes.label: heart | tear | anguish
nodes.sentence: But my heart is torn with anguish, and my spirit is tossed with desire, for I have heard of thy deeds of prowess, and how thou fearest neither Deev nor lion, neither leopard nor crocodile, and how thy hand is swift to strike, and how thou didst venture alone into Mazanderan, and how wild asses are devoured of thee, and how the earth groaneth under the tread of thy feet, and how men perish at thy blows, and how even the eagle dareth not swoop down upon her prey when she beholdeth thy sword.

nodes.label: thing | tell | i
nodes.sentence: These things and more have they told unto me, and mine eyes have yearned to look upon thy face.

nodes.label: ∅ | hath | ∅
nodes.sentence: And now hath God brought thee within the gates of my father, and I am come to say unto thee that I am thine if thou wilt hear me, and if thou wilt not, none other will I espouse.

nodes.label: ∅ | consider | pehliva
nodes.sentence: And consider, O Pehliva, how that love hath obscured mine understanding and withdrawn me from the bosom of discretion, yet peradventure God will grant unto me a son like to thee for strength and valour, to whom shall be given the empire of the world.

nodes.label: i | lead | ∅
nodes.sentence: And if thou wilt listen unto me, I will lead forth before thee

nodes.label: rostam | regard | she
nodes.sentence: Now while this moon of beauty was yet speaking, Rostam regarded her.

nodes.label: he | see | ∅
nodes.sentence: And he saw that she was fair, and that wisdom abode in her mind; and when he heard of Rakhsh, his spirit was decided within him, and he held that this adventure could not end save gloriously.

nodes.label: he | send | mubid
nodes.sentence: So he sent a Mubid unto the King and demanded the hand of Tahmineh from her father.

nodes.label: king | rejoice | ∅
nodes.sentence: And the King, when he heard the news, was rejoiced, and gave his daughter unto the Pehliva, and they concluded an alliance according to custom and the rites.

nodes.label: man | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: And all men, young and old, within the house and city of the King were glad at this alliance, and called down blessings upon Rostam.

nodes.label: rostam | take | onyx
nodes.sentence: Now Rostam, when he was alone with the Peri-faced, took from his arm an onyx that was known unto all the world.

nodes.label: he | give | it
nodes.sentence:
And he gave it to her, and said-

"Cherish this jewel, and if Heaven cause thee to give birth unto a daughter, fasten it within her locks, and it will shield her from evil; but if it be granted unto thee to bring forth a son, fasten it upon his arm, that he may wear it like his father.

nodes.label: he | be | sam
nodes.sentence: And he shall be strong as Keriman, of stature like unto Sam the son of Neriman, and of grace of speech like unto Zal, my father.

nodes.label: faced | be | ∅
nodes.sentence:
"

The Peri-faced, when she had heard these words, was glad in his presence.

nodes.label: ∅ | come | they
nodes.sentence: But when the day was passed there came in unto them the King her father, and he told Rostam how that tidings of Rakhsh were come unto his ears, and how that the courser would shortly be within the gates.

nodes.label: rostam | fill | longing
nodes.sentence: And Rostam, when he heard it, was filled with longing after his steed, and when he knew that he was come he hastened forth to caress him.

nodes.label: he | fasten | saddle
nodes.sentence: And with his own hands he fastened the saddle, and gave thanks unto Ormuzd, who had restored his joy between his hands.

nodes.label: he | know | ∅
nodes.sentence: Then he knew that the time to depart was come.

nodes.label: he | open | arm
nodes.sentence: And he opened his arms and took unto his heart Tahmineh the fair of face, and he bathed her cheek with his tears and covered her hair with kisses.

nodes.label: he | fling | he
nodes.sentence: Then he flung him upon Rakhsh, and the swift-footed bare him quickly from out of her sight.

nodes.label: tahmineh | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: And Tahmineh was sorrowful exceedingly, and Rostam too was filled with thoughts as he turned him back into Zaboulistan.

nodes.label: he | ponder | adventure
nodes.sentence: And he pondered this adventure in his heart, but to no man did he speak of what he had seen or done.

nodes.label: man | call | he
nodes.sentence: Now when nine moons had run their course there was born unto Tahmineh a son in the likeness of his father, a babe whose mouth was filled with smiles, wherefore men called him Sohrab.

nodes.label: he | be | child
nodes.sentence: And when he numbered but one month he was like unto a child of twelve, and when he numbered five years he was skilled in arms and all the arts of war, and when ten years were rolled above his head there was none in the land that could resist him in the games of strength.

nodes.label: he | come | mother
nodes.sentence: Then he came before his mother and spake words of daring.

nodes.label: he | said- | ∅
nodes.sentence:
And he said-

"Since I am taller and stouter than my peers, teach unto me my race and lineage, and what I shall say when men ask me the name of my sire.

nodes.label: i | strike | thee
nodes.sentence: But if thou refuse an answer unto my demands, I will strike thee out from the rolls of the living."

nodes.label: she | smile | spirit
nodes.sentence: When Tahmineh beheld the ardour of her son, she smiled in her spirit because that his fire was like to that of his father.

nodes.label: she | open | mouth
nodes.sentence:
And she opened her mouth and said-

"Hear my words, O my son, and be glad in thine heart, neither give way in thy spirit to anger.

nodes.label: art | descend | art
nodes.sentence: For thou art the offspring of Rostam, thou art descended from the seed of Sam and Zal, and Neriman was thy forefather.

nodes.label: ∅ | hold | none
nodes.sentence: And since God made the world it hath held none like unto Rostam, thy sire.

nodes.label: she | show | letter
nodes.sentence:
"

Then she showed to him a letter written by the Pehliva, and gave to him the gold and jewels Rostam had sent at his birth.

nodes.label: she | spake | ∅
nodes.sentence:
And she spake and said-

"Cherish these gifts with gratitude, for it is thy father who hath sent them.

nodes.label: ∅ | remember | son
nodes.sentence: Yet remember, O my son, that thou close thy lips concerning these things; for Turan groaneth under the hand of Afrasiyab, and he is foe unto Rostam the glorious.

nodes.label: he | seek | ∅
nodes.sentence: If, therefore, he should learn of thee, he would seek to destroy the son for hatred of the sire.

nodes.label: he | demand | thee
nodes.sentence: Moreover, O my boy, if Rostam learned that thou wert become a mountain of valour, perchance he would demand thee at my hands, and the sorrow of thy loss would crush the heart of thy mother."

nodes.label: sohrab | reply | ∅
nodes.sentence: But Sohrab replied, "Nought can be hidden upon earth for aye.

nodes.label: ∅ | know | deed
nodes.sentence: To all men are known the deeds of Rostam, and since my birth be thus noble, wherefore hast thou kept it dark from me so long?

nodes.label: i | give | ∅
nodes.sentence: I will go forth with an army of brave Turks and lead them unto Iran, I will cast Kai Kawous from off his throne, I will give to Rostam the crown of the Kaianides, and together we will subdue the land of Turan, and Afrasiyab shall be slain by my hands.

nodes.label: i | mount | throne
nodes.sentence: Then will I mount the throne in his stead.

nodes.label: shalt | call | queen
nodes.sentence: But thou shalt be called Queen of Iran.

nodes.label: king | rule | world
nodes.sentence: for since Rostam is my father and I am his son no other kings shall rule in this world, for to us alone behoveth it to wear the crowns of might.

nodes.label: i | pant | longing
nodes.sentence: And I pant in longing after the battlefield, and I desire that the world should behold my prowess.

nodes.label: horse | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: But a horse is needful unto me, a steed tall and strong of power to bear me, for it beseemeth me not to go on foot before mine enemies.

nodes.label: tahmineh | rejoice | soul
nodes.sentence:
"

Now Tahmineh, when she had heard the words of this boy, rejoiced in her soul at his courage.

nodes.label: she | bid | guardian
nodes.sentence: So she bade the guardians of the flocks lead out the horses before Sohrab her son.

nodes.label: they | do | ∅
nodes.sentence: And they did as she had bidden, and Sohrab surveyed the steeds, and tested their strength like as his father had done before him of old, and he bowed them under his hand, and he could not be satisfied.

nodes.label: he | seek | steed
nodes.sentence: And thus for many days did he seek a worthy steed.

nodes.label: one | come | he
nodes.sentence: Then one came before him and told of a foal sprung from Rakhsh, the swift of foot.

nodes.label: sohrab | hear | tiding
nodes.sentence: When Sohrab heard the tidings he smiled, and bade that the foal be led before him.

nodes.label: he | test | it
nodes.sentence: And he tested it and found it to be strong.

nodes.label: he | saddle | it
nodes.sentence:
So he saddled it and sprang upon its back and cried, saying-

"Now that I own a horse like thee, the world shall be made dark to many.

nodes.label: he | make | ready
nodes.sentence:
"

Then he made ready for war against Iran, and the nobles and warriors flocked around him.

nodes.label: sohrab | come | grandsire
nodes.sentence: And when all was in order Sohrab came before his grandsire and craved his counsel and his aid to go forth into the land of Iran and seek out his father.

nodes.label: king | deem | they
nodes.sentence: And the King of Samengan, when he heard these wishes, deemed them to be just, and he opened the doors of his treasures without stint and gave unto Sohrab of his wealth, for he was filled with pleasure at this boy.

nodes.label: he | invest | sohrab
nodes.sentence: And he invested Sohrab with all the honours of a King, and he bestowed on him all the marks of his good pleasure.

nodes.label: man | bring | news
nodes.sentence: Meantime a certain man brought news unto Afrasiyab that Sohrab was making ready an army to fall upon Iran, and to cast Kai Kawous from off his throne.

nodes.label: he | tell | afrasiyab
nodes.sentence: And he told Afrasiyab how the courage and valour of Sohrab exceeded words.

nodes.label: afrasiyab | hide | contentment
nodes.sentence: And Afrasiyab, when he heard this, hid not his contentment, and he called before him Hooman and Barman, the doughty.

nodes.label: he | bid | ∅
nodes.sentence: Then he bade them gather together an army and join the ranks of Sohrab, and he confided to them his secret purpose, but he enjoined them to tell no man thereof.

nodes.label: ∅ | hath | hand
nodes.sentence:
For he said-

"Into our hands hath it been given to settle the course of the world.

nodes.label: it | know | i
nodes.sentence: For it is known unto me that Sohrab is sprung from Rostam the Pehliva, but from Rostam must it be hidden who it is that goeth out against him, then peradventure he will perish by the hands of this young lion, and Iran, devoid of Rostam, will fall a prey into my hands.

nodes.label: we | subdue | sohrab
nodes.sentence: Then will we subdue Sohrab also, and all the world will be ours.

nodes.label: grief | bring | he
nodes.sentence: But if it be written that Sohrab fall under the hand of Tehemten, then the grief he shall endure when he shall learn that he hath slain his son will bring him to the grave for sorrow.

nodes.label: ∅ | spake | afrasiyab
nodes.sentence:
"

So spake Afrasiyab in his guile, and when he had done unveiling his black heart he bade the warriors depart unto Samengan.

nodes.label: they | bare | gift
nodes.sentence: And they bare with them gifts of great price to pour before the face of Sohrab.

nodes.label: they | bare | letter
nodes.sentence: And they bare also a letter filled with soft words.

nodes.label: afrasiyab | laud | sohrab
nodes.sentence: And in the letter Afrasiyab lauded Sohrab for his resolve, and told him how that if Iran be subdued the world would henceforth know peace, for upon his own head would he place the crown of the Kaianides; and Turan, Iran, and Samengan should be as one land.

nodes.label: he | rejoice | ∅
nodes.sentence: When Sohrab had read this letter, and saw the gifts and the aid sent out to him, he rejoiced aloud, for he deemed that now none could withstand his might.

nodes.label: he | cause | ∅
nodes.sentence: So he caused the cymbals of departure to be clashed, and the army made them ready to go forth.

nodes.label: sohrab | lead | they
nodes.sentence: Then Sohrab led them into the land of Iran.

nodes.label: track | mark | ∅
nodes.sentence: And their track was marked by desolation and destruction, for they spared nothing that they passed.

nodes.label: they | spread | fire
nodes.sentence: And they spread fire and dismay abroad, and they marched on unstayed until they came unto the White Castle, the fortress wherein Iran put its trust.

nodes.label: guardian | name | hujir
nodes.sentence: Now the guardian of the castle was named Hujir, and there lived with him Gustahem the brave, but he was grown old, and could aid no longer save with his counsels.

nodes.label: ∅ | abode | gurdafrid
nodes.sentence: And there abode also his daughter Gurdafrid, a warlike maid, firm in the saddle, and practised in the fight.

nodes.label: he | come | ∅
nodes.sentence: Now when Hujir beheld from afar a dusky cloud of armed men he came forth to meet them.

nodes.label: sohrab | draw | sword
nodes.sentence: And Sohrab, when he saw him, drew his sword, and demanded his name, and bade him prepare to meet his end.

nodes.label: he | taunt | he
nodes.sentence: And he taunted him with rashness that he was come forth thus unaided to stand against a lion.

nodes.label: hujir | answer | sohrab
nodes.sentence: But Hujir answered Sohrab with taunts again, and vowed that he would sever his head from his trunk and send it for a trophy unto the Shah.

nodes.label: sohrab | smile | ∅
nodes.sentence: Yet Sohrab only smiled when he heard these words, and he challenged Hujir to come near.

nodes.label: they | meet | combat
nodes.sentence: And they met in combat, and wrestled sore one with another, and stalwart were their strokes and strong; but Sohrab overcame Hujir as though he were an infant, and he bound him and sent him captive unto Hooman.

nodes.label: ∅ | grieve | ∅
nodes.sentence: And Gurdafrid too, when she learned it, was grieved, but she was ashamed also for the fate of Hujir.

nodes.label: she | take | ∅
nodes.sentence: So she took forth burnished mail and clad herself therein, and she hid her tresses under a helmet of Roum, and she mounted a steed of battle and came forth before the walls like to a warrior.

nodes.label: she | utter | cry
nodes.sentence: And she uttered a cry of thunder, and flung it amid the ranks of Turan, and she defied the champions to come forth to single combat.

nodes.label: none | come | ∅
nodes.sentence: And none came, for they beheld her how she was strong, and they knew not that it was a woman, and they were afraid.

nodes.label: sohrab | step | ∅
nodes.sentence:
But Sohrab, when he saw it, stepped forth and said-

"I will accept thy challenge, and a second prize will fall into my hands.

nodes.label: he | gird | himself
nodes.sentence:
"

Then he girded himself and made ready for the fight.

nodes.label: maid | rain | arrow
nodes.sentence: And the maid, when she saw he was ready, rained arrows upon him with art, and they fell quick like hail, and whizzed about his head; and Sohrab, when he saw it, could not defend himself, and was angry and ashamed.

nodes.label: he | cover | head
nodes.sentence: Then he covered his head with a shield and ran at the maid.

nodes.label: she | drop | bow
nodes.sentence: But she, when she saw him approach, dropped her bow and couched a lance, and thrust at Sohrab with vigour, and shook him mightily, and it wanted little

nodes.label: she | throw | he
nodes.sentence: and she would have thrown him from his seat.

nodes.label: sohrab | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: And Sohrab was amazed, and his wrath knew no bounds.

nodes.label: he | run | gurdafrid
nodes.sentence: Then he ran at Gurdafrid with fury, and seized the reins of her steed, and caught her by the waist, and tore her armour, and threw her upon the ground.

nodes.label: she | draw | sword
nodes.sentence: Yet ere he could raise his hand to strike her, she drew her sword and shivered his lance in twain, and leaped again upon her steed.

nodes.label: she | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: And when she saw that the day was hers, she was weary of further combat, and she sped back unto the fortress.

nodes.label: sohrab | give | rein
nodes.sentence: But Sohrab gave rein unto his horse, and followed after her in his great anger.

nodes.label: he | catch | she
nodes.sentence: And he caught her, and seized her, and tore the helmet from off her head, for he desired to look upon the face of the man who could withstand the son of Rostam.

nodes.label: ∅ | roll | coil
nodes.sentence: And lo! when he had done so, there rolled forth from the helmet coils of dusky hue, and Sohrab beheld it was a woman that had overcome him in the fight.

nodes.label: he | confound | ∅
nodes.sentence: And he was confounded.

nodes.label: he | said- | ∅
nodes.sentence:
But when he had found speech he said-

"If the daughters of Iran are like to thee, and go forth unto battle, none can stand against this land.

nodes.label: he | take | cord
nodes.sentence:
"

Then he took his cord and threw it about her, and bound her in its snare, saying-

"Seek not to escape me, O moon of beauty, for never hath prey like unto thee fallen between my hands.

nodes.label: gurdafrid | turn | face
nodes.sentence:
"

Then Gurdafrid, full of wile, turned unto him her face that was unveiled, for she beheld no other means of safety, and she said unto him-

"O hero without flaw, is it well that thou shouldest seek to make me captive, and show me unto the army?

nodes.label: ∅ | behold | combat
nodes.sentence: For they have beheld our combat, and that I overcame thee, and surely now they will gibe when they learn that thy strength was withstood by a woman.

nodes.label: it | beseem | thee
nodes.sentence: Better would it beseem thee to hide this adventure, lest thy cheeks have cause to blush because of me.

nodes.label: ∅ | let | ∅
nodes.sentence: Therefore let us conclude a peace together.

nodes.label: castle | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: The castle shall be thine, and all it holds; follow after me then, and take possession of thine own.

nodes.label: sohrab | beguile | ∅
nodes.sentence:
"

Now Sohrab, when he had listened, was beguiled by her words and her beauty, and he said-

"Thou dost wisely to make peace with me, for verily these walls could not resist my might."

nodes.label: he | follow | she
nodes.sentence: And he followed after her unto the heights of the castle, and he stood with her before its gates.

nodes.label: gustahem | open | portal
nodes.sentence: And Gustahem, when he saw them, opened the portal, and Gurdafrid stepped within the threshold, but when Sohrab would have followed after her she shut the door upon him.

nodes.label: sohrab | see | ∅
nodes.sentence: Then Sohrab saw that she had befooled him, and his fury knew no bounds.

nodes.label: he | fall | prey
nodes.sentence: But ere he was recovered from his surprise she came out upon the battlements and scoffed at him, and counselled him to go back whence he was come; for surely, since he could not stand against a woman, he would fall an easy prey before Rostam, when the Pehliva should have learned that robbers from Turan were broken into the land.

nodes.label: sohrab | make | madder
nodes.sentence: And Sohrab was made yet madder for her words, and he departed from the walls in his wrath, and rode far in his anger, and spread terror in his path.

nodes.label: he | vow | ∅
nodes.sentence: And he vowed that he would yet bring the maid into subjection.

nodes.label: ∅ | be | none
nodes.sentence: And he told how Hujir had been bound, and how the fortress was like to fall into the hands of the enemy; for there were none to defend it save only his daughter and himself and he craved the Shah to come to their aid.

nodes.label: sohrab | make | ∅
nodes.sentence: Albeit when the day had followed yet again upon the night, Sohrab made ready his host to fall upon the castle.

nodes.label: he | find | ∅
nodes.sentence: But when he came near thereto he found it was empty, and the doors thereof stood open, and no warriors appeared upon its walls.

nodes.label: he | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: And he was surprised, for he knew not that in the darkness the inmates were fled by a passage that was hidden under the earth.

nodes.label: he | search | building
nodes.sentence:
And he searched the building for Gurdafrid, for his heart yearned after her in love, and he cried aloud-

"Woe, woe is me that this moon is vanished behind the clouds!"

nodes.label: he | afflict | ∅
nodes.sentence: Now when Kai Kawous had gotten the writing of Gustahem, he was sore afflicted and much afraid, and he called about him his nobles and asked their counsels.

nodes.label: he | said- | ∅
nodes.sentence:
And he said-

"Who shall stand against this Turk?

nodes.label: doth | liken | he
nodes.sentence: For Gustahem doth liken him in power unto Rostam, and saith he resembleth the seed of Neriman."

nodes.label: warrior | cry | accord
nodes.sentence: Then the warriors cried with one accord, "Unto Rostam alone can we look in this danger!"

nodes.label: kawous | hearken | voice
nodes.sentence: And Kai Kawous hearkened to their voice, and he called for a scribe and dictated unto him a letter.

nodes.label: he | write | pehliva
nodes.sentence: And he wrote unto his Pehliva, and invoked the blessings of Heaven upon his head, and he told him all that was come to pass, and how new dangers threatened Iran, and how to Rostam alone could he look for help in his trouble.

nodes.label: he | recall | all
nodes.sentence: And he recalled unto Tehemten all that he had done for him in the days that were gone by, and he entreated him once again to be his refuge.

nodes.label: he | said- | ∅
nodes.sentence:
And he said-

"When thou shalt receive this letter, stay not to speak the word that hangeth upon thy lips; and if thou bearest roses in thy hands, stop not to smell them, but haste thee to help us in our need.

nodes.label: kawous | send | geew
nodes.sentence:
"

Then Kai Kawous sent forth Geew with this writing unto Zaboulistan, and bade him neither rest nor tarry until he should stand before the face of Rostam.

nodes.label: he | said- | ∅
nodes.sentence:
And he said-

"When thou hast done my behest, turn thee again unto me; neither abide within the courts of the Pehliva, nor linger by the roadside."

nodes.label: geew | do | ∅
nodes.sentence: And Geew did as the Shah commanded, and took neither food nor rest till he set foot within the gates of Rostam.

nodes.label: rostam | greet | he
nodes.sentence: And Rostam greeted him kindly, and asked him of his mission; and when he had read the writing of the Shah, he questioned Geew concerning Sohrab.

nodes.label: i | marvel | ∅
nodes.sentence:
For he said-

"I should not marvel if such an hero arose in Iran, but that a warrior of renown should come forth from amid the Turks, I cannot believe it.

nodes.label: i | have | son
nodes.sentence: I have myself a son in Samengan, but he is yet an infant, and his mother writeth to me that he rejoiceth in the sports of his age, and though he be like to become a hero among men, his time is not yet come to lead forth an army.

nodes.label: they | betook | themselves
nodes.sentence: So they betook themselves again unto clubs, and Sohrab aimed at Rostam with might and smote him, and Rostam reeled beneath the stroke, and bit his lips in agony.

nodes.label: he | stand | youth
nodes.sentence: Then Sohrab vaunted his advantage, and-bade Rostam go and measure him with his equals; for though his strength be great, he could not stand against a youth.

nodes.label: they | go | way
nodes.sentence: So they went their ways, and Rostam fell upon the men of Turan, and spread confusion far and wide among their ranks; and Sohrab raged along the lines of Iran, and men and horses fell under his hands.

nodes.label: rostam | be | soul
nodes.sentence: And Rostam was sad in his soul, and he turned with sorrow into his camp.

nodes.label: sohrab | work | ∅
nodes.sentence: But when he saw the destruction Sohrab had wrought his anger was kindled, and he reproached the youth, and challenged him to come forth yet again to single combat.

nodes.label: rostam | go | kawous
nodes.sentence: Then Rostam went before Kai Kawous and told him of this boy of valour, and he prayed unto Ormuzd that He would give him strength to vanquish his foe.

nodes.label: he | make | house
nodes.sentence: Yet he made ready also his house lest he should fall in the fight, and he commanded that a tender message be borne unto Rudabeh, and he sent words of comfort unto Zal, his father.

nodes.label: sohrab | laud | might
nodes.sentence: And Sohrab, too, in his camp lauded the might of Rostam, and he said how the battle had been sore, and how his mind had misgiven him of the issue.

nodes.label: he | spake | hooman
nodes.sentence:
And he spake unto Hooman, saying-

"My mind is filled with thoughts of this aged man, mine adversary, for it would seem unto me that his stature is like unto mine, and that I behold about him the tokens that my mother recounted unto me.

nodes.label: heart | goeth | ∅
nodes.sentence: And my heart goeth out towards him, and I muse if it be Rostam, my father.

nodes.label: ∅ | tell | i
nodes.sentence: Wherefore, I beseech thee, tell unto me how this may be.

nodes.label: hooman | answer | ∅
nodes.sentence:
"

But Hooman answered and said, "Oft have I looked upon the face of Rostam in battle, and mine eyes have beheld his deeds of valour; but this man in no wise resembleth him, nor is his manner of wielding his club the same.

nodes.label: thing | spake | hooman
nodes.sentence:
"

These things spake Hooman in his vileness, because that Afrasiyab had enjoined him to lead Sohrab into destruction.

nodes.label: sohrab | hold | peace
nodes.sentence: And Sohrab held his peace, but he was not wholly satisfied.

nodes.label: rostam | stride | spot
nodes.sentence: Now when the day had begun to lighten the sky and clear away the shadows, Rostam and Sohrab strode forth unto the midway spot that stretched between the armies.

nodes.label: ∅ | sohrab | bare
nodes.sentence:
And Sohrab bare in his hands a mighty club, and the garb of battle was upon him; but his mouth was full of smiles, and he asked of Rostam how he had rested, and he said-

"Wherefore hast thou prepared thy heart for battle?

nodes.label: i | beg | ∅
nodes.sentence: Cast from thee, I beg, this mace and sword of vengeance, and let us doff our armour, and seat ourselves together in amity, and let wine soften our angry deeds.

nodes.label: conflict | be | impure
nodes.sentence: For it seemeth unto me that this conflict is impure.

nodes.label: heart | speak | thee
nodes.sentence: And if thou wilt listen to my desires, my heart shall speak to thee of love, and I will make the tears of shame spring up into thine eyes.

nodes.label: ∅ | tell | name
nodes.sentence: And for this cause I ask thee yet again, tell me thy name, neither hide it any longer, for I behold that thou art of noble race.

nodes.label: it | seem | i
nodes.sentence: And it would seem unto me that thou art Rostam, the chosen one, the Lord of Zaboulistan, the son of Zal, the son of Sam the hero."

nodes.label: rostam | answer | ∅
nodes.sentence: But Rostam answered, "O hero of tender age, we are not come forth to parley but to combat, and mine ears are sealed against thy words of lure.

nodes.label: i | be | man
nodes.sentence: I am an old man, and thou art young, but we are girded for battle, and the Master of the world shall decide between us."

nodes.label: sohrab | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: Then Sohrab said, "O man of many years, wherefore wilt thou not listen to the counsel of a stripling?

nodes.label: i | desire | ∅
nodes.sentence: I desired that thy soul should leave thee upon thy bed, but thou hast elected to perish in the combat.

nodes.label: ∅ | let | ∅
nodes.sentence: That which is ordained it must be done, therefore let us make ready for the conflict.

nodes.label: they | make | ∅
nodes.sentence:
"

So they made them ready, and when they had bound their steeds they fell upon each other, and the crash of their encounter was heard like thunder throughout the camps.

nodes.label: they | measure | strength
nodes.sentence: And they measured their strength from the morning until the setting of the sun.

nodes.label: sohrab | seize | rostam
nodes.sentence: And when the day was about to vanish, Sohrab seized upon Rostam by the girdle and threw him upon the ground, and kneeled upon him, and drew forth his sword from his scabbard, and would have severed his head from his trunk.

nodes.label: rostam | know | ∅
nodes.sentence: Then Rostam knew that only wile could save him.

nodes.label: he | open | mouth
nodes.sentence:
So he opened his mouth and said-

"O young man, thou knowest not the customs of the combat.

nodes.label: it | write | law
nodes.sentence: It is written in the laws of honour that he who overthroweth a brave man for the first time should not destroy him, but preserve him for fight a second time, then only is it given unto him to kill his adversary.

nodes.label: sohrab | listen | word
nodes.sentence:
"

And Sohrab listened to Rostam's words of craft and stayed his hand, and he let the warrior go, and because that the day was ended he sought to fight no more, but turned him aside and chased the deer until the night was spent.

nodes.label: hooman | come | he
nodes.sentence: Then came to him Hooman, and asked of the adventures of the day.

nodes.label: sohrab | tell | he
nodes.sentence: And Sohrab told him how he had vanquished the tall man, and how he had granted him freedom.

nodes.label: hooman | reproach | he
nodes.sentence:
And Hooman reproached him with his folly, and said-

"Alas, young man, thou didst fall into a snare, for this is not the custom among the brave.

nodes.label: wilt | fall | hand
nodes.sentence: And now perchance thou wilt yet fall under the hands of this warrior.

nodes.label: sohrab | abash | ∅
nodes.sentence:
"

Sohrab was abashed when he heard the words of Hooman, but he said-

"Be not grieved, for in an hour we meet again in battle, and verily he will not stand a third time against my youthful strength.

nodes.label: rostam | go | brook
nodes.sentence:
"

Now while Sohrab was thus doing, Rostam was gone beside a running brook, and laved his limbs, and prayed to God in his distress.

nodes.label: he | entreat | ormuzd
nodes.sentence: And he entreated of Ormuzd that He would grant him such strength that the victory must be his.

nodes.label: ormuzd | hear | he
nodes.sentence: And Ormuzd heard him, and gave to him such strength that the rock whereon Rostam stood gave way under his feet, because it had not the power to bear him.

nodes.label: rostam | see | ∅
nodes.sentence: Then Rostam saw it was too much, and he prayed yet again that part thereof be taken from him.

nodes.label: ormuzd | listen | voice
nodes.sentence: And once more Ormuzd listened to his voice.

nodes.label: rostam | turn | he
nodes.sentence: Then when the time for combat was come, Rostam turned him to the meeting-place, and his heart was full of cares and his face of fears.

nodes.label: sohrab | come | giant
nodes.sentence:
But Sohrab came forth like a giant refreshed, and he ran at Rostam like to a mad elephant, and he cried with a voice of thunder-

"O thou who didst flee from battle, wherefore art thou come out once more against me?

nodes.label: i | say | word
nodes.sentence: But I say unto thee, this time shall thy words of guile avail thee nought.

nodes.label: rostam | seize | misgiving
nodes.sentence:
"

And Rostam, when he heard him, and looked upon him, was seized with misgiving, and he learned to know fear.

nodes.label: he | pray | ormuzd
nodes.sentence: So he prayed to Ormuzd that He would restore to him the power He had taken back.

nodes.label: he | suffer | sohrab
nodes.sentence: But he suffered not Sohrab to behold his fears, and they made them ready for the fight.

nodes.label: he | close | sohrab
nodes.sentence: And he closed upon Sohrab with all his new-found might, and shook him terribly, and though Sohrab returned his attacks with vigour, the hour of his overthrow was come.

nodes.label: ∅ | take | he
nodes.sentence: For Rostam took him by the girdle and hurled him unto the earth, and he broke his back like to a reed, and he drew forth his sword to sever his body.

nodes.label: sohrab | know | ∅
nodes.sentence:
Then Sohrab knew it was the end, and he gave a great sigh, and writhed in his agony, and he said-

"That which is come about, it is my fault, and henceforward will my youth be a theme of derision among the people.

nodes.label: i | speed | glory
nodes.sentence: But I sped not forth for empty glory, but I went out to seek my father; for my mother had told me by what tokens I should know him, and I perish for longing after him.

nodes.label: ∅ | have | ∅
nodes.sentence: And now have my pains been fruitless, for it hath not been given unto me to look upon his face.

nodes.label: i | say | thee
nodes.sentence: Yet I say unto thee, if thou shouldest become a fish that swimmeth in the depths of the ocean, if thou shouldest change into a star that is concealed in the farthest heaven, my father would draw thee forth from thy hiding-place, and avenge my death upon thee when he shall learn that the earth is become my bed.

nodes.label: ∅ | be | rostam
nodes.sentence: For my father is Rostam the Pehliva, and it shall be told unto him how that Sohrab his son perished in the quest after his face."

nodes.label: rostam | hear | word
nodes.sentence: When Rostam heard these words his sword fell from out of his grasp, and he was shaken with dismay.

nodes.label: ∅ | break | groan
nodes.sentence: And there broke from his heart a groan as of one whose heart was racked with anguish.

nodes.label: earth | become | eye
nodes.sentence: And the earth became dark before his eyes, and he sank down lifeless beside his son.

nodes.label: he | cry | sohrab
nodes.sentence: But when he had opened his eyes once more, he cried unto Sohrab in the agony of his spirit.

nodes.label: he | said- | token
nodes.sentence:
And he said-

"Bearest thou about thee a token of Rostam, that I may know that the words which thou speakest are true?

nodes.label: misery | be | ∅
nodes.sentence:
When Sohrab heard these words his misery was boundless, and he cried-

"If thou art indeed my father, then hast thou stained thy sword in the life-blood of thy son.

nodes.label: thou | didst | it
nodes.sentence: And thou didst it of thine obstinacy.

nodes.label: i | seek | ∅
nodes.sentence: For I sought to turn thee unto love, and I implored of thee thy name, for I thought to behold in thee the tokens recounted of my mother.

nodes.label: i | appeal | heart
nodes.sentence: But I appealed unto thy heart in vain, and now is the time gone by for meeting.

nodes.label: ∅ | regard | jewel
nodes.sentence: Yet open, I beseech thee, mine armour, and regard the jewel upon mine arm.

nodes.label: ∅ | be | onyx
nodes.sentence: For it is an onyx given unto me by my father, as a token whereby he should know me.

nodes.label: rostam | do | ∅
nodes.sentence:
"

Then Rostam did as Sohrab bade him, and he opened his mail and saw the onyx; and when he had seen it he tore his clothes in his distress, and he covered his head with ashes.

nodes.label: tear | run | eye
nodes.sentence: And the tears of penitence ran from his eyes, and he roared aloud in his sorrow.

nodes.label: sohrab | be | remedy
nodes.sentence:
But Sohrab said-

"It is in vain, there is no remedy.

nodes.label: it | write | doubtless
nodes.sentence: Weep not, therefore, for doubtless it was written that this should be."

nodes.label: noble | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: Now when the sun was set, and Rostam returned not to the camp, the nobles of Iran were afraid, and they went forth to seek him.

nodes.label: they | raise | wailing
nodes.sentence: And when they were gone but a little way they came upon Rakhsh, and when they saw that he was alone they raised a wailing, for they deemed that of a surety Rostam was perished.

nodes.label: they | go | ∅
nodes.sentence:
And they went and told Kai Kawous thereof, and he said-

"Let Tus go forth and see if this indeed be so, and if Rostam be truly fallen, let the drums call men unto battle that we may avenge him upon this Turk."

nodes.label: sohrab | turn | father
nodes.sentence:
Now Sohrab, when he beheld afar off the men that were come out to seek Rostam, turned to his father and said-

"I entreat of thee that thou do unto me an act of love.

nodes.label: ∅ | let | ∅
nodes.sentence: Let not the Shah fall upon the men of Turan, for they came not forth in enmity to him but to do my desire, and on my head alone resteth this expedition.

nodes.label: i | desire | ∅
nodes.sentence: Wherefore I desire not that they should perish when I can defend them no longer.

nodes.label: i | come | thunder
nodes.sentence: As for me, I came like the thunder and I vanish like the wind, but perchance it is given unto us to meet again above.

nodes.label: rostam | promise | ∅
nodes.sentence:
"

Then Rostam promised to do the desires of Sohrab.

nodes.label: he | go | man
nodes.sentence: And he went before the men of Iran, and when they beheld him yet alive they set up a great shout, but when they saw that his clothes were torn, and that he bare about him the marks of sorrow, they asked of him what was come to pass.

nodes.label: he | tell | they
nodes.sentence: Then he told them how he had caused a noble son to perish.

nodes.label: they | grieve | he
nodes.sentence: And they were grieved for him, and joined in his wailing.

nodes.label: he | bid | one
nodes.sentence: Then he bade one among them go forth into the camp of Turan, and deliver this message unto Hooman.

nodes.label: he | send | word
nodes.sentence:
And he sent word unto him, saying-

"The sword of vengeance must slumber in the scabbard.

nodes.label: art | return | ∅
nodes.sentence: Thou art now leader of the host, return, therefore, whence thou camest, and depart across the river

nodes.label: day | fall | ∅
nodes.sentence: many days be fallen.

nodes.label: i | fight | more
nodes.sentence: As for me, I will fight no more, yet neither will I speak unto thee again, for thou didst hide from my son the tokens of his father, of thine iniquity thou didst lead him into this pit."

nodes.label: rostam | turn | he
nodes.sentence: Then when he had thus spoken, Rostam turned him yet again unto his son.

nodes.label: noble | go | he
nodes.sentence: And the nobles went with him, and they beheld Sohrab, and heard his groans of pain.

nodes.label: rostam | be | himself
nodes.sentence: And Rostam, when he saw the agony of the boy, was beside himself, and would have made an end of his own life, but the nobles suffered it not, and stayed his hand.

nodes.label: rostam | remember | he
nodes.sentence: Then Rostam remembered him that Kai Kawous had a balm mighty to heal.

nodes.label: he | pray | ∅
nodes.sentence: And he prayed Goodarz go before the Shah, and bear unto him a message of entreaty from Rostam his servant.

nodes.label: ∅ | recall | benefit
nodes.sentence:
And he said-

"O Shah, if ever I have done that which was good in thy sight, if ever my hand have been of avail unto thee, recall now my benefits in the hour of my need, and have pity upon my dire distress.

nodes.label: i | pray | thee
nodes.sentence: Send unto me, I pray thee, of the balm that is among thy treasures, that my son may be healed by thy grace."

nodes.label: goodarz | outstrip | whirlwind
nodes.sentence: And Goodarz outstripped the whirlwind in his speed to bear unto the Shah this message.

nodes.label: heart | harden | ∅
nodes.sentence: But the heart of Kai Kawous was hardened, and he remembered not the benefits he had received from Rostam, and he recalled only the proud words that he had spoken before him.

nodes.label: he | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: And he was afraid lest the might of Sohrab be joined to that of his father, and that together they prove mightier than he, and turn upon him.

nodes.label: he | shut | ear
nodes.sentence: So he shut his ear unto the cry of his Pehliva.

nodes.label: goodarz | bear | answer
nodes.sentence:
And Goodarz bore back the answer of the Shah, and he said-

"The heart of Kai Kawous is flinty, and his evil nature is like to a bitter gourd that ceaseth never to bear fruit.

nodes.label: i | counsel | thee
nodes.sentence: Yet I counsel thee, go before him thyself, and see if peradventure thou soften this rock.

nodes.label: rostam | do | ∅
nodes.sentence: And Rostam in his grief did as Goodarz counselled, and turned to go before the Shah, but he was not come before him ere a messenger overtook him, and told unto him that Sohrab was departed from the world.

nodes.label: rostam | set | wail
nodes.sentence: Then Rostam set up a wailing such as the earth hath not heard the like of, and he heaped reproaches upon himself, and he could not cease from plaining the son that was fallen by his hands.

nodes.label: he | cry | ∅
nodes.sentence:
And he cried continually-

"I that am old have killed my son.

nodes.label: i | uproot | boy
nodes.sentence: I that am strong have uprooted this mighty boy.

nodes.label: i | lay | head
nodes.sentence: I have torn the heart of my child, I have laid low the head of a Pehliva.

nodes.label: armour | try | battle
nodes.sentence:
"

Then he made a great fire, and flung into it his tent of many colours, and his trappings of Roum, his saddle, and his leopard-skin, his armour well tried in battle, and all the appurtenances of his throne.

nodes.label: he | stand | ∅
nodes.sentence: And he stood by and looked on to see his pride laid in the dust.

nodes.label: he | tear | flesh
nodes.sentence:
And he tore his flesh, and cried aloud-

"My heart is sick unto death."

nodes.label: he | command | ∅
nodes.sentence: Then he commanded that Sohrab be swathed in rich brocades of gold worthy of his body.

nodes.label: he | make | ∅
nodes.sentence: And when they had enfolded him, and Rostam learned that the Turanians had quitted the borders, he made ready his army to return unto Zaboulistan.

nodes.label: noble | march | bier
nodes.sentence: And the nobles marched before the bier, and their heads were covered with ashes, and their garments were torn.

nodes.label: drum | shatter | ∅
nodes.sentence: And the drums of the war-elephants were shattered, and the cymbals broken, and the tails of the horses were shorn to the root, and all the signs of mourning were abroad.

nodes.label: he | know | ∅
nodes.sentence: Now Zal, when he saw the host returning thus in sorrow, marvelled what was come about; for he beheld Rostam at their head, wherefore he knew that the wailing was not for his son.

nodes.label: he | come | rostam
nodes.sentence: And he came before Rostam and questioned him.

nodes.label: rostam | lead | he
nodes.sentence: And Rostam led him unto the bier and showed unto him the youth that was like in feature and in might unto Sam the son of Neriman, and he told him all that was come to pass, and how this was his son, who in years was but an infant, but a hero in battle.

nodes.label: rudabeh | come | ∅
nodes.sentence: And Rudabeh too came out to behold the child, and she joined her lamentations unto theirs.

nodes.label: they | build | sohrab
nodes.sentence: Then they built for Sohrab a tomb like to a horse's hoof, and Rostam laid him therein in a chamber of gold perfumed with ambergris.

nodes.label: he | cover | he
nodes.sentence: And he covered him with brocades of gold.

nodes.label: house | grow | grave
nodes.sentence: And when it was done, the house of Rostam grew like to a grave, and its courts were filled with the voice of sorrow.

nodes.label: joy | enter | heart
nodes.sentence: And no joy would enter into the heart of Rostam, and it was long before he held high his head.

nodes.label: news | spread | turan
nodes.sentence: Meantime the news spread even unto Turan, and there too did all men grieve and weep for the child of prowess that was fallen in his bloom.

nodes.label: king | tear | vestment
nodes.sentence: And the King of Samengan tore his vestments, but when his daughter learned it she was beside herself with affliction.

nodes.label: tahmineh | cry | son
nodes.sentence: And Tahmineh cried after her son, and bewailed the evil fate that had befallen him, and she heaped black earth upon her head, and tore her hair, and wrung her hands, and rolled on the ground in her agony.

nodes.label: mouth | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: And her mouth was never weary of plaining.

nodes.label: she | cause | ∅
nodes.sentence: Then she caused the garments of Sohrab to be brought unto her, and his throne and his steed.

nodes.label: she | regard | they
nodes.sentence: And she regarded them, and stroked the courser and poured tears upon his hoofs, and she cherished the robes as though they yet contained her boy, and she pressed the head of the palfrey unto her breast, and she kissed the helmet that Sohrab had worn.

nodes.label: she | cut | tail
nodes.sentence: Then with his sword she cut off the tail of his steed and set fire unto the house of Sohrab, and she gave his gold and jewels unto the poor.

nodes.label: breath | depart | body
nodes.sentence: And when a year had thus rolled over her bitterness, the breath departed from out her body, and her spirit went forth after Sohrab her son.
